commit.message:
Add Seventh Generation EasyDose Ultra Power+ Clean Scent (#397)

## Description

Add detergent product: **Seventh Generation EasyDose Ultra Power+ Clean
Scent**

Ultra-concentrated EasyDose liquid (USA). 55 ingredients — 12 base + 43
plant-derived fragrances. Requires 43 new enum entries and a significant
expansion of `Scents.ts`.

**`Ingredient.ts`** — 43 new entries (alphabetical, with JSDoc):
- Essential oils: `AmyrisBalsamiferaBarkOil`,
`ArtemisiaPallensFlowerOil`, `BoswelliaCarteriOil`,
`BulnesiaSarmientoiWoodOil`, `CanangaOdorataFlowerOil`,
`CitrusAurantifoliaLimeOil`, `CitrusAurantiumAmaraLeafTwigOil`,
`CitrusAurantiumBergamiaFruitOil`, `CitrusLimonPeelOil`,
`CoriandrumSativumFruitOil`, `CymbopogonMartiniPalmarosaOil`,
`EucalyptusGlobusLeafOil`, `EugeniaCaryphyllusLeafOil`,
`FoeniculumVulgareOil`, `IlliciumVerumFruitSeedOil`,
`JuniperusMexicanaCedarOil`, `JuniperusVirginianaCedarwoodOil`,
`LavandulaHybridaLavandinOil`, `MyroxylonPereiraeOil`,
`PelargoniumGraveolensFlowerOil`, `PogostemonCablinOil`,
`RosmarinusOfficinalisLeafOil`, `SalviaSclaraeOil`
- Fragrance aroma chemicals / isolates: `BetaPinene`, `Cinnamal`,
`DimethylHeptenal`, `FerulGalbaniferaResinOil`, `GammaDecalactone`,
`GammaNonalactone`, `Geraniol`, `Heliotropine`, `IsoamylSalicylate`,
`Linalool`, `PhenethylAlcohol`, `ThreeHexenol`, `Vanillin`
- Extracts / resins: `CitrusAurantiumDulcisFruitExtract`,
`CistusLadaniferusResin`, `CistusLadaniferusResinAbsolute`,
`PogostemonCablinLeafExtract`, `StyraxTonkinensisResinExtract`,
`LaminariaDigitataExtract`
- Updated `CaprylylCaprylGlucoside` JSDoc: added synonym `Octyl/Decyl
Glucoside`

**`Scents.ts`** — expanded to include all new fragrance ingredients,
plus ingredients added from concurrent PRs merged into main
(`HexylCinnamal`, `MenthaArvensisLeafOil`,
`RosaDamascenaFlowerExtract`).

**`seventh-generation-easydose-ultra-power-plus-clean-scent.ts`** — new
profile, 55 ingredients, `countriesAvailable: ['USA']`.

**`profiles/index.ts`** — export added in alphabetical order between the
two other Seventh Generation entries.

## Unknowns / Ambiguities

- `FerulGalbaniferaResinOil` key name follows the issue spec; enum value
string matches label text (`Ferula Galbanifula`) per policy.

## Source(s)

- **Primary source:** Packaging image — Target store, USA
- **Date accessed:** 2026-03-17

the worker runs correctly via npx (tsx --import doesn't resolve
tsconfig paths in npx cache directories). App and test files keep
using @/ since Next.js and Vitest handle them natively.

- CI: oven-sh/setup-bun -> actions/setup-node

commit.message:
feat: add CLI-based Atlassian Confluence REST API toolkit

Add dual-mode support via MODE env var (gui/cli/test):
- MODE=gui (default): existing HTTP server with browser UI
- MODE=cli: Confluence REST API v2 CLI toolkit
- MODE=test: internal unit tests (base64 encoder)

CLI features:
- acli-style command hierarchy: confluence <resource> <action> [flags]
- 10 resources: auth, page, space, search, comment, label, version,
  attachment, property, bulk
- Auth: env vars (CONFLUENCE_SITE/EMAIL/TOKEN) or stored (auth login)
- Output: compact JSON default, --pretty for indented, --verbose for debug
- Pagination: --limit N and --all flags with cursor-based auto-pagination
- Error handling: structured JSON on stderr, exit codes 0-4
- HTTPS natively via WasmEdge QuickJS built-in rustls

Test infrastructure (TDD):
- 18 TAP-format test suites in tests/
- Mock Confluence server (tests/mock-server.js)
- Shared test helpers (tests/helpers.sh)

Also:
- Fix HTTP method case (WasmEdge QuickJS lowercase methods)
- Add -- separator in Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT for CLI flag passthrough
- Add CLI mode docs to README.md and README-ko.md
- Add commented CLI service example in docker-compose.yml

commit.message:
Add support for async thunk methods in GetNativeCodeInfo (#124354)

The runtime by default caches the async thunk when looking up runtime
async methods in the methoddef map:
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/0c7ef81aa7d25dcac00eb81d156e03f4c0ca396b/src/coreclr/vm/clsload.cpp#L2804-L2810
This causes `DacDbiInterfaceImpl::GetNativeCodeInfo` to incorrectly
return data for the async thunk and not the actual method.

commit.message:
perf: replace per-face BRepExtrema in TryFindClosestFace with Extrema_ExtPS (#146)

Callgrind insight 5: TryFindClosestFace (used by SurfaceNormal) called
BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape(vertex, face) per candidate face, allocating
NCollection_Sequence<BRepExtrema_SolutionElem> and internal bookkeeping
on each call, driving NCollection_BaseSequence::ClearSeq to 1.04% of
total instructions.

Pre-build BRepAdaptor_Surface per face in fFaceBoundsCache at
ComputeBounds() time. In TryFindClosestFace, use Extrema_ExtPS on the
pre-built surface adaptor instead of BRepExtrema. A BRepExtrema fallback
handles degenerate cases where Extrema_ExtPS finds no solutions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: route ExactDistanceTo* through AABB face prefilter (#144)

* docs: add PR-history lessons to AGENTS.md

Analyzed all 130 pull requests and their reviewer feedback to identify
recurring mistakes made by AI agents. Condense these into concrete rules
across new and expanded sections of AGENTS.md.

Additions to existing sections:
- §1 License: shebang must not precede SPDX header; cmake-format guards
  required for CMake SPDX headers; Jinja2 uses HTML comment style; scripts
  under .github/ covered by SPDX check; Geant4 example files keep their
  original license block with ignore-paths in spdx.yml
- §2 Include Style: add rule against relying on transitive includes
- §4 CMake: PRIVATE for include dirs, PUBLIC for link libs; do not use
  check_cxx_compiler_flag for sanitizer flag probing
- §5 Testing: yaml-cpp required (hand-rolled parsers rejected twice);
  per-fixture CTest split required; disabled tests need programmatic skip
  with explanatory comment and issue link; xfail reclassification must use
  narrow code allowlists; fixture provenance must document all parameters;
  regenerate and commit STEP files after changing generation scripts
- §6 CI: eic_xl:nightly (not eic_ci); cvmfs prerequisite step required;
  no branches: filter on pull_request trigger; git diffs must be generated
  outside the Apptainer container
- §7 Documentation: docstrings must accurately describe implementation;
  docs must be updated in same PR as implementation; code examples must be
  syntactically valid; British spelling required (visualisation not
  visualization)
- §9 Geometry: BRepBndLib::AddOptimal not Add; DistanceToIn/Out returns
  lower bound; GetPointOnSurface must use OCCT tessellation; internal types
  must be private; G4Polyhedra uses circumradius not apothem
- §11 Code Quality: clang-format Standard must be c++20; CSS/JS in separate
  files; codespell notes on Geant4 command names vs prose misspellings

New sections:
- §12 Sanitizers: scope env vars to sanitizer job; narrow suppression scopes;
  no check_cxx_compiler_flag for sanitizer detection
- §13 Report and Script Conventions: scripts in .github/scripts/; shared
  utility module; parsers must be updated with benchmark format changes;
  America/New_York timezone
- §14 Examples: keep original Geant4 license; STEP fixtures centered at
  origin; add Geant4 UI command words to .codespell-ignore instead of
  changing the commands

Also add 'inactivate' to .codespell-ignore since it is a valid Geant4 UI
command (/process/inactivate) quoted in AGENTS.md as an example.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: fix two issues from code review in AGENTS.md

- Correct shebang rule: executable scripts may have a shebang on line 1
  followed immediately by the SPDX header (the required layout for .sh
  files in the repository). The previous wording incorrectly implied
  shebangs must never appear before the SPDX header.

- Clarify xfail error code allowlist rule: point to the canonical source
  (fixture_validation.hh / fixture_validation.cc) for the current list of
  demotable codes, and note that the examples given are illustrative rather
  than exhaustive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: route ExactDistanceTo* queries through AABB face prefilter

Insights 1 and 5 from callgrind analysis: ExactDistanceToOut and
ExactDistanceToIn both created BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape on the
full shape/compound per query, triggering O(N_faces) calls to
TopExp::MapShapes and BRepBndLib::Add on every DistanceToOut(p)
and DistanceToIn(p) invocation.

Route both through TryFindClosestFace which uses the pre-built
fFaceBoundsCache (AABB per face, constructed once at shape load)
to prune candidates before calling BRepExtrema on individual faces.
For a point near a single face this reduces O(N_faces) BRepExtrema
calls to O(1).

Remove the now-unused DistanceFromPointToShape free function,
fFaceCompound member, and its TopoDS_Compound.hxx include since
ExactDistanceToOut no longer needs the face compound.

Charts: Derive default legend shape from chart type in composition API (#47671)

* Charts: Derive default legend shape from chart type in composition API

When using the composition API (e.g. <LineChart.Legend />), the Legend
component now automatically derives the default shape from the
registered chart type. Previously it always fell back to 'rect'
(BaseLegend default), ignoring the chart type.

Shape defaults by chart type:
- line -> 'line'
- bar -> 'rect'
- pie / pie-semi-circle / leaderboard -> 'circle'

An explicit shape prop still overrides the derived default.

Fixes CHARTS-192

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix lint: use Testing Library methods and fix Prettier formatting

Replace direct DOM access (querySelectorAll/querySelector) with
document.body.innerHTML string matching and Testing Library queries
(screen.getByRole, screen.getAllByTestId) to satisfy the
testing-library/no-node-access ESLint rule. Fix Prettier formatting
in legend.tsx and legend.test.tsx.

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use Extract<LegendShape> instead of inline string literal union

Derive the value type from visx's LegendShape type using
Extract<LegendShape<unknown, unknown>, string> to stay in sync
with the upstream type definition.

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add positive rect assertion to bar chart shape test

Assert that the bar chart legend renders a rect shape (visx renders
rects as divs with a background style) rather than only asserting
the absence of line/circle shapes.

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix type import

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(charts): Add 'line' to legend shape Storybook control options

The legendShape control only listed 'circle' and 'rect', so users
couldn't select 'line' in Storybook despite it being a valid shape
supported by visx LegendShape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Use Partial<Record> for defaultShapeByChartType

Only a few chart types have mapped legend shapes, so Partial
accurately reflects that unknown chart types return undefined.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Add ChartType union type for chart registration

Replace plain `string` with a `ChartType` union type
('bar' | 'conversion-funnel' | 'leaderboard' | 'line' | 'pie' |
'pie-semi-circle') in ChartRegistration, useChartRegistration, and
the legend shape mapping. This catches typos at compile time and
makes the set of supported chart types explicit.
